serving.description: 1 set meal (ramen bowl + sides + lemon tea)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal consisting of a bowl of soy milk tomato seaweed ramen (approximately 500 g) plus sides and a house-made iced or hot lemon tea (approximately 150 g), totalling approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central: Miso soup with tofu and seaweed (SR Legacy), soy milk (SR Legacy ID 16120), wheat ramen noodles (SR Legacy ID 20113), and tomato-based broth components; proportionally weighted for a soy milk tomato seaweed ramen bowl
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for soy milk tomato seaweed ramen set exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional composition modelling: approximately 45% cooked wheat ramen noodles, 30% soy milk tomato broth, 10% seaweed (wakame), 10% lemon tea, 5% other garnishes. Per-100g values are derived from USDA SR Legacy component entries and weighted accordingly. Values represent a reasonable central estimate; actual restaurant preparation may vary.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; wheat noodles / ramen noodle GI reference (GI ~47–52 for boiled wheat noodles); soy milk GI ~34 (University of Sydney GI Database)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for soy milk tomato seaweed ramen set. GI estimated as weighted composite: boiled wheat noodles GI ~50 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283) weighted at ~55% carbohydrate contribution; soy milk GI ~34 (University of Sydney GI Database) weighted at ~25%; tomato broth and seaweed contribute negligible glycemic load. Composite GI estimated at approximately 48, classified as low (≤55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 70.2) / 100 ≈ 33.7, rounded to 33.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from broth and seaweed
hypertension.guidance: This ramen set contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g, yielding an estimated 2015 mg per full serving; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium broth or consume a smaller portion to stay within the recommended 1500–2000 mg daily sodium limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from wheat ramen noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: The soy milk tomato broth lowers the overall glycemic index to approximately 48 (low GI), but the full set meal delivers a glycemic load of approximately 34, which is significant; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a half-portion of noodles, pair with additional seaweed or vegetables, and monitor postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat; soy isoflavones may be cardioprotective
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The soy milk base provides a low saturated fat profile (approximately 0.2 g per 100 g) and soy isoflavones that may modestly support LDL reduction; however, the sodium content from the broth warrants attention for individuals with cardiovascular disease, and portion control is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soy Milk Tomato Seaweed Ramen Set is a Japanese-inspired fusion ramen dish served as a set meal at Flavours by Saute (Funan, Singapore). The dish features wheat ramen noodles cooked in a creamy soy milk and tomato broth, topped with seaweed (typically wakame or nori), and served as a complete set that includes sides and a house-made iced or hot lemon tea. The soy milk broth imparts a mild, slightly sweet, and umami-rich flavour, while the tomato adds acidity and depth. The seaweed contributes a subtle oceanic note and provides iodine, calcium, and dietary fibre. The overall texture is silky from the soy milk, with tender noodles and soft rehydrated seaweed. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in energy (approximately 68 kcal per 100 g), provides plant-based protein from soy milk (approximately 3.2 g per 100 g), and is low in saturated fat. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 48 (low GI), making it a relatively moderate glycemic option compared to standard ramen. Sodium content is elevated due to the broth, which is a consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The soy milk base may offer modest cardiovascular benefits through isoflavone content. This set meal is suitable for vegetarians (assuming no animal-derived broth additives) and is dairy-free. It is not suitable for individuals with gluten intolerance due to wheat noodles, nor for those with soy allergies.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific soy milk tomato seaweed ramen set exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional composition modelling from USDA SR Legacy component entries. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for wheat noodles and soy milk. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA SR Legacy component entries (wheat ramen noodles, soy milk, wakame seaweed, tomato broth, lemon tea); actual restaurant values may differ. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for estimation. 3. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for estimation. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by formulation; plant-based soy milk and vegetable broth contain no industrially produced trans fats. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero; this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients assumed in the base formulation. 6. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero; this dish contains no animal-derived ingredients in its base formulation and soy milk used is assumed to be unfortified. 7. GI set to 48 (low, ≤55) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat noodles (GI ~50) and soy milk (GI ~34); gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 8. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (48 × 70.2 g carbohydrate per 650 g serving) / 100 ≈ 33.8. 9. The input record classified this food under 'Beverages' food_group; this has been corrected to 'Mixed' as the item is a complete set meal, not a beverage. 10. Multilingual names provided for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Japanese given the Japanese-fusion cuisine context served in Singapore. 11. is_halal flag set to false as halal certification status for Flavours by Saute is not confirmed from approved sources.
